rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CIPRIANO CAGUIMBAGA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate is set for 3/18/24. If a petition is not filed by 3/4/24, either for final distribution or to request more time, an appearance will be required on 3/18/24. When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KALVIN M. MA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/21/23. (No earlier available date.) 1) Courtesy copies are file stamped, SINGLE-sided, hard copies. 2) Decedent died on 5/22/21. Please provide authority for the Court to accept a disclaimer made on 3/8/23. [Probate Code Section 279(b)(2).] 3) Does Michelle Nicole Chiu have any issue? ("Issue" is not limited to just children. [Probate Code Section 50.]) 4) A petition is either supplemented to provide additional information or amended to correct information. [CRC 7.3.] A "declaration" is not very meaningful, and the "declaration" filed on 3/14/23 was actually an amendment to the petition, because the fee was changed. However, since the administrator is presumed to be the only interested party - if Michelle Nicole Chiu does not have any children or more remote issue - the mischaracterization of the pleading might be waived. 5) Notice must also be given to the Director of Health Care Services, if the decedent was the surviving spouse of a person who received Medi-Cal benefits. [Probate Code Section 9202(a).] 6) Item 5 of the I&A and the Corrected I&A was not answered. Deadline for delivering the courtesy copy of any responsive paper(s) to the assigned examiner is 6/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YUNG CHIH YUEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/7/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (2) Exhibit B to the petition does not appear to be quite correct and the fee base is not set forth. [CRC 7.705.] (3) Attorney's costs are not fully itemized, showing what was sent to whom via Federal Express or what was filed with the Court, etc. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Mishi McCaig? The administrator is maintaining all of the estate funds well over the FDIC limit at one bank)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HITOSHI OHE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/21/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (2) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain). [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] A percentage alone is not sufficient. (3) Furthermore, the petition does not set forth how much is in the estate. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

Petition lists multiple nephews and nieces but each one's parent who is/was related to the settlor is not listed. 4. A copy of the current title of the Clearlake property to be submitted.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID R. JOHNSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: UPDATED TENTATIVE RULING: Continued to 05/31/2023 by Judicial Officer after review. 1. Petitioner to provide more recent case law in support of this petition. Need to explain why admitting this will is necessary. Petition does not provide information why petitioner cannot exercise the power but for the court admitting this will. 2. The court is not inclined to probate the will without appointing a personal representative in this case because it is not clear to the court if no property is and will be subject to the administration. Also, such allegation must be made by personal representative, not from the petitioner. 3. The court is not inclined to probate the will without ascertaining that the court can fulfill the supervisory responsibilities (ex. protecting potential creditors and beneficiary/heir) in administration proceedings.
